articles.title: China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch
articles.summary:
A number of new Chinese state-led and commercial rockets are set for debuts and fresh first stage recovery attempts in the coming weeks and months.
The post China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Journey to the centre of a galaxy cluster
ESA Greenlights Two Budget-Friendly Earth Observation Missions
articles.summary:
The European Space Agency has greenlit its HiBiDiS and SOVA-S Scout missions, with development led by SITAEL and OHB Czechspace, respectively. These missions are required to progress from initial approval to launch within three years, with costs capped at €35 million. On 20 May, the agency announced that it had selected its next two Scout […]
The post ESA Greenlights Two Budget-Friendly Earth Observation Missions appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: New Glenn rocket explodes on Cape Canaveral pad
articles.summary:
A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad at Cape Canaveral, Florida, during a May 28 hotfire test, destroying the rocket and causing extensive pad damage.
The post New Glenn rocket explodes on Cape Canaveral pad appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explodes during prelaunch testing at Cape Canaveral
articles.summary: The explosion happened at 9 p.m. EDT (0100 UTC) during a static fire test of the rocket at Launch Complex 36 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station. Blue Origin was slated to launch a mission for Amazon Leo as soon as June 4.

articles.title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
articles.summary: Radar data from an agricultural area in South Africa, shown in a vivid color palette, reveal crop types and how they changed during the Southern Hemisphere’s growing season.

articles.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Rocket Explodes During Pre-Launch Test
articles.summary: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket exploded during a pre-launch test this evening. The company said all personnel are safe and accounted for following the “anomaly.” The company was conducting a […]

articles.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket just exploded during a static fire test
articles.summary: New Glenn was due to play a starring role in NASA's Artemis Program.
